race.name: Granfondo La Guadeloupe
race.tagline: Tropical UCI Gran Fondo qualifier with Caribbean climbs and sea views.

climate.overview: Tropical Caribbean climate in December, warm and lush during northern winter with reduced heat and humidity.

terrain.overview: Undulating coastal roads with major mountain climbs on Basse-Terre side.

logistics.explanation: International airport access but remote Caribbean location adds travel complexity.

length.explanation: Solid 124km Gran Fondo distance with meaningful challenge.

technicality.explanation: Road event with steady climbs, low descending demands.

elevation.explanation: 2883m over 124km provides serious but accessible suffering.

climate.explanation: Ideal December warmth without extreme heat.

adventure.explanation: Exotic tropical paradise with volcano views and beaches.

prestige.explanation: UCI qualifier status elevates new event.

race_quality.explanation: Well-organized with FFC/UCI standards and signature climb.

experience.explanation: Combine racing with Caribbean vacation and expo.

community.explanation: New event, building Caribbean cycling scene.

value.explanation: High experience for tropical UCI points.

expenses.explanation: Flights and lodging premium for island destination.

biased_opinion.overview: Brand new UCI Gran Fondo in a stunning tropical setting—perfect for winter training with Worlds qualifier spots and post-race beach recovery. The Col des Mamelles climb delivers the pain amid paradise vistas; pair it with the discovery ride for full island immersion. Logistics favor Europeans, but worth the trip for adventure seekers.

final_verdict.one_liner: Exotic UCI qualifier blending steep climbs and Caribbean allure.
final_verdict.full_verdict: Granfondo La Guadeloupe stands out as a fresh 2025 addition to the calendar, offering 124km/2883m of tropical road suffering with top-25% age-group qualification to 2026 Worlds in Japan. Signature 13.5km Col des Mamelles (4km at >8.5%) anchors a scenic Basse-Terre loop from Capesterre-Belle-Eau. Ideal for amateurs seeking prestige points in paradise, with expo, non-competitive 80km option, and abundant tourism. Travel via Pointe-à-Pitre airport; French infrastructure ensures quality support.

logistics.overview: Accessible via major international airport; registrations via sportsnconnect.com until Nov 30, 2025; 86 hotels plus alternatives.
logistics.registration_notes: Open to amateurs 19+ with FFC license or medical cert; UCI rules apply.

history.overview: New 2025 event, first edition Dec 6-7; UCI Gran Fondo World Series qualifier organized by Team Cama CCD and OD'ASS as Caribbean license holders.

course_description.character: Coastal undulations to major climb, then rolling west with smaller ascent; tropical mountain roads with sea and volcano views.

suffering_zones.label: Col des Mamelles: 13.5km at 4.3% avg (4km >8.5%, max 620m)

non_negotiables: UCI qualifier: top 25% per age group to 2026 Worlds
French Caribbean: prepare for tropical logistics
